raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command that finds .java files, computes md5, groups duplicates. Use find, md5sum, sort, uniq -d? Actually need list of duplicate md5 sums. So: find /testbed -type f -name '*.java' -print0 | xargs -0 md5sum | sort | uniq -d -f 1? md5sum outputs hash and filename. We want duplicate hashes. So: find ... | xargs md5sum | awk '{print $1}' | sort | uniq -d. That's list of duplicate md5 sums. So final command: find /testbed -type f -name '*.java' -print0 | xargs -0 md5sum | awk '{print $1}' | sort | uniq -d.
